description: Quiz questions about Base Layer 2 blockchain and Ethereum scaling

questions.question: What is Base, really?
questions.explainer: Base is a Layer 2 blockchain that processes transactions off Ethereum mainnet for faster speeds and lower costs.

questions.question: Who built this magic?
questions.explainer: Coinbase built Base as their Layer 2 solution to provide users with faster and cheaper Ethereum transactions.

questions.question: Base's mainnet buddy?
questions.explainer: Base is built on top of Ethereum, inheriting its security while providing enhanced performance and lower fees.

questions.question: Why use Base?
questions.explainer: Base offers significantly cheaper transaction fees compared to Ethereum mainnet while maintaining security and compatibility.

questions.question: Base's secret sauce?
questions.explainer: Optimistic Rollups assume transactions are valid by default, only verifying when challenged, enabling faster processing.

questions.question: Built on what tech?
questions.explainer: Base is built using Optimism's OP Stack technology, leveraging proven optimistic rollup infrastructure for scalability.

questions.question: What can you do?
questions.explainer: Deploy smart contracts using familiar Ethereum tools like Solidity, Hardhat, and Remix with full EVM compatibility.

questions.question: Security provided by?
questions.explainer: Base inherits Ethereum's robust security model, ensuring transactions are protected by the world's largest blockchain network.

questions.question: Network type?
questions.explainer: Layer 2 scaling solution that processes transactions off-chain while settling final states on Ethereum mainnet.

questions.question: Why is Base cool?
questions.explainer: Base transactions cost significantly less than Ethereum Layer 1, making DeFi and dApps more accessible to users.

questions.question: Wallet compatibility?
questions.explainer: Any Ethereum-compatible wallet works with Base, including MetaMask, Coinbase Wallet, and hardware wallets.

questions.question: Decentralization style?
questions.explainer: Base follows progressive decentralization, gradually transitioning from centralized operations to community governance.

questions.question: Coding language?
questions.explainer: Solidity smart contracts work seamlessly on Base, enabling developers to use existing Ethereum development skills.

questions.question: Block speed?
questions.explainer: Base produces blocks every 2 seconds, providing much faster transaction confirmation than Ethereum's 12-second blocks.

questions.question: Bridge to use?
questions.explainer: Base Bridge is the official bridge for moving assets between Ethereum mainnet and Base Layer 2 network.

questions.question: Gas token?
questions.explainer: ETH is used for gas fees on Base, maintaining consistency with Ethereum's economic model and user familiarity.

questions.question: Address style?
questions.explainer: Base uses Ethereum-style addresses, allowing seamless migration of existing Ethereum accounts and contracts.

questions.question: Dev framework?
questions.explainer: Hardhat and other Ethereum development frameworks work natively on Base, requiring no special modifications.

questions.question: Security model?
questions.explainer: Base inherits Ethereum's battle-tested security, protected by thousands of validators and proven consensus mechanisms.

questions.question: Deployment method?
questions.explainer: Use standard Ethereum deployment tools like Remix, Hardhat, and Foundry without any modifications or special setup.

questions.question: Base's focus?
questions.explainer: Base focuses on bringing the next billion users onchain with accessible, low-cost blockchain interactions.

questions.question: Transaction finality?
questions.explainer: Optimistic rollups provide fast transaction finality with dispute resolution mechanisms for security guarantees.

questions.question: Challenge period?
questions.explainer: 7-day challenge period allows anyone to dispute invalid transactions before they're finalized on Ethereum.

questions.question: Ecosystem member?
questions.explainer: Base is a core member of the Ethereum ecosystem, extending its capabilities rather than competing with it.

questions.question: Base's superpower?
questions.explainer: Lower transaction costs make DeFi, NFTs, and dApps accessible to users who couldn't afford Ethereum mainnet fees.

questions.question: Scaling method?
questions.explainer: Base batches multiple transactions off-chain and submits compressed data to Ethereum for efficient scaling.

questions.question: Ethereum relationship?
questions.explainer: Base extends Ethereum as a Layer 2 solution, enhancing its capabilities rather than replacing or competing with it.

questions.question: Dev can build?
questions.explainer: Developers can build any type of decentralized application, from DeFi protocols to NFT marketplaces and social apps.

questions.question: Contract deployment?
questions.explainer: Smart contract deployment works exactly like Ethereum, using the same tools, syntax, and deployment processes.

questions.question: Base's purpose?
questions.explainer: Base scales Ethereum by processing transactions faster and cheaper while maintaining security and decentralization.

questions.question: Transaction processing?
questions.explainer: Transactions are processed on Base then batched and submitted to Ethereum mainnet for final settlement.

questions.question: Development style?
questions.explainer: Base development is open-source, allowing community contributions and transparent development processes.
